column1: # E. MWO FACULAR COUNTS FOR BOTH POLES CALIBRATED TO WSO BY ANDRES MUNOZ-JARAMILLO
# VERSION 2 - Please indicate version used in scientific publications in case of changes.
# Faculae counted by hand on the best 5 images during the periods of maximum pole coverage
# (August 15-September 15 for the North pole and February 15-March 15 for the South pole)
# and averaged.  Standard deviation has been turned into standard error by dividing it by sqrt(5).
# Years with multiple observations have been averaged as well as their their standard errors.
# Calibration between campaigns was made taking advantage of the overlaps between them.   All campaigns
# were standardized to the values of the 3rd campaign in order to reduce error propagation.
# Calibration factors used were:
# 1. To turn 1st campaign values into 2nd campaign:  0.8733 +/- 0.0343
# 2. To turn 2nd campaign values into 3rd campaign:  0.7293 +/- 0.1918
# 3. To turn 4th campaign values into 3rd campaign:  1.6601 +/- 0.1861
# Unification of all campaigns is performed by averaging overlapping years between the calibrated campaigns
# as well as their standard errors.
# Calibration to WSO field is performed by using WSO data only during periods of maximum polar coverage
# and the overlap between the unified WSO facular count and WSO data.
# The calibration factor used was:
# To turn MWO facular counts to WSO magnetic field:  (0.0852 +/- 0.0069) Gauss
# Original papers reporting the different MWO data reduction campaigns are:
# 1st Campaign:
# Sheeley N. R. Jr. 1964 ApJ 140 731
# Sheeley N. R. Jr. 1966 ApJ 144 723
# 2nd Campaign:
# Sheeley N. R. Jr. 1976 J. Geophys. Res. 81 3462
# 3rd Campagin:
# Sheeley N. R. Jr. 1991 ApJ 374 386
# 4th Campagin:
# Sheeley N. R. Jr. 2008 ApJ 680 1553
# Details on the consolidation and calibration of the different campaigns can be found in:
# A. Munoz-Jaramillo
# CALIBRATING 100 YEARS OF POLAR FACULAE MEASUREMENTS: IMPLICATIONS
# FOR THE EVOLUTION OF THE HELIOSPHERIC MAGNETIC FIELD
# ApJ
# Paper available from:
# https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2012ApJ...753..146M/abstract
# 1st (7th) column indicates time of measurement for MWO northern (southern) hemisphere data.
# 2nd (8th) column contains MWO facular count for the North (South) pole calibrated to WSO polar field.  Units are in Gauss
# 3rd (9th) column contains standard error of the mean (SEM) in MWO facular count for the North (South) pole.  Units are in Gauss
# 4th (10th) column indicates time of measurement for WSO northern (southern) hemisphere data.
# 5th (11th) column contains WSO polar field.  Units are in Gauss
# 6th (12th) column contains estimated standard error for WSO data based on equivalent measurements on MDI and HMI.  Units are in Gauss
# Created on 16-Oct-2023
N MWO Date
